discussion_posts.name: shabri87
discussion_posts.content:
For input:
["http://psn.wlyby.edu/wvoz","http://psn.wlyby.edu/shez","http://psn.wlyby.edu/upkr","http://psn.wlyby.edu/ubmr","http://psn.wlyby.edu/apgb","http://psn.wlyby.edu/sbin","http://psn.wlyby.edu/inmj","http://cpq.jkvox.tech/mjkb","http://lqr.shmtu.tech/rsvw","http://ylk.fubmn.com/ypyh"]
[[0,8],[1,6],[1,7],[1,4],[3,3],[3,4],[3,7],[4,1],[4,0],[4,3],[5,5],[5,8],[5,5],[5,0],[6,8],[7,2],[7,7],[7,4],[10,7],[10,4],[10,3],[10,4]]
"http://psn.wlyby.edu/ubmr"


Expected is:["http://psn.wlyby.edu/ubmr","http://psn.wlyby.edu/apgb","http://psn.wlyby.edu/shez","http://psn.wlyby.edu/wvoz","http://psn.wlyby.edu/upkr","http://psn.wlyby.edu/inmj"]
While expected assumed by leetcode is: ["http://psn.wlyby.edu/apgb","http://psn.wlyby.edu/inmj","http://psn.wlyby.edu/shez","http://psn.wlyby.edu/ubmr","http://psn.wlyby.edu/wvoz"] 
should have had one more URL "http://psn.wlyby.edu/upkr" which is missing.

discussion_posts.name: mstaricka
discussion_posts.content:
In order to get the solution to run in Kotlin I needed to add an import to my solution:
```
import java.util.Random
```

Apparently they are instantiating ```Random()``` behind the scenes which is not working as ```kotlin.random.Random()``` requires a seed parameter. Adding the import forces the Java version of Random to be used.

discussion_posts.name: Manukyanq
discussion_posts.content:
IMHO this is too easy for anyone who ever used DFS/BFS at least one time. 

tip for JS/TS coders use JS built in URL class to get hostname without any extra logic: 

 ```js
  const { hostname } = new URL(startUrl)
```

discussion_posts.name: sculd
discussion_posts.content: I think the test case has a bug. It should excludes the urls whose host ITSELF does not belong to the start url. But instead it excludes those whose intermediate path is not part of the same host even though the url itself has the same host as the start.
